channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Ebola is spreading faster in eastern Congo than it can be tracked, as deaths pass 700
item.description: Eighty percent of new Ebola cases in eastern Congo are emerging from unknown chains of transmission, according to WHO, a sign the outbreak is spreading faster than health officials can track.
item.content:encoded:
Eighty percent of new Ebola cases in eastern Congo are emerging from unknown chains of transmission, according to WHO, a sign the outbreak is spreading faster than health officials can track.

(Image credit: Dirole Lotsima Dieudonne)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rep. Ro Khanna on being detained by Israeli settlers in the West Bank
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., about his recent trip to the occupied West Bank and temporary detention by Israeli settlers and soldiers.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., about his recent trip to the occupied West Bank and temporary detention by Israeli settlers and soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Aslam

item.title: U.S. to reinstate Hormuz blockade. And, states sue over Paramount-Warner deal
item.description: The United States plans to reinstate a blockade over the Strait of Hormuz today. And, several states are suing to stop the massive Paramount-Warner Bros. merger.
item.content:encoded:
The United States plans to reinstate a blockade over the Strait of Hormuz today. And, several states are suing to stop the massive Paramount-Warner Bros. merger.

(Image credit: -)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The U.S. is back to blockading Iran as the Strait of Hormuz standoff escalates
item.description: The U.S. military says it's begun its blockade of ships entering or exiting Iran's ports, as Iran vows to assert its own control over the critical Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military says it's begun its blockade of ships entering or exiting Iran's ports, as Iran vows to assert its own control over the critical Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Death toll from a Bangkok music bar fire rises to 30, dozens remain in hospital
item.description: More than 70 people were injured, with 24 of them still in critical condition, according to a statement by the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration.
item.content:encoded:
More than 70 people were injured, with 24 of them still in critical condition, according to a statement by the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration.

(Image credit: Sakchai Lalit)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Pakistan urges restraint as its Mideast deal unravels
item.description: Pakistan urges restraint as Iran and the U.S. clash and a memorandum of understanding unravels.
item.content:encoded: Pakistan urges restraint as Iran and the U.S. clash and a memorandum of understanding unravels.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: People watching the World Cup during work hours is leading to a drop in productivity
item.description: The global economy may be suffering from lost productivity as people get swept by World Cup fever, a survey shows. This small business owner would know. She's spent most of her days watching matches.
item.content:encoded: The global economy may be suffering from lost productivity as people get swept by World Cup fever, a survey shows. This small business owner would know. She's spent most of her days watching matches.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: The U.S. strikes Iran after Trump announces a renewed blockade and tolls in Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. and Iran are fighting for control of the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a return to all-out war after agreeing to a ceasefire last month.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Iran are fighting for control of the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a return to all-out war after agreeing to a ceasefire last month.

(Image credit: Razieh Poudat)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire grows more distant. And, Congress faces a consequential week
item.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire over the weekend amid tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. And, Congress returns from recess facing tight deadlines.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire over the weekend amid tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. And, Congress returns from recess facing tight deadlines.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Sam Neill, known for 'Jurassic Park' and 'The Piano,' dies at 78, his family says
item.description: In 2023, Neill disclosed he had been diagnosed with angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma, a rare type of non-Hodgkin lymphoma. Neill died on Monday in Sydney, according to a statement posted to the actor's social media page.
item.content:encoded:
In 2023, Neill disclosed he had been diagnosed with angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma, a rare type of non-Hodgkin lymphoma. Neill died on Monday in Sydney, according to a statement posted to the actor's social media page.

(Image credit: Richard Shotwell/AP Photo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
